what_it_does: Implements a single, persistent, surface-independent conversation store and turn loop for Vi. It stores messages in localStorage, notifies subscribers, sends composed prompts to window.claude.complete, parses inline [[verb|k=v]] action directives from Vi replies, and applies those actions through global project registries such as MS_FEEDBACK, MS_IDENTITY, MS_MEMORY, MS_DECISIONS, MS_ARTIFACTS, and MS_PRODUCE.
observations: Messages are persisted to localStorage under the key ms_vi_chat_v1, capped to the last 40 entries.
State changes are broadcast through a simple Set-based pub/sub pattern.
send() builds the prompt from MS_VI_BRAIN.systemPrompt and appends a PROTOCOL string only when opts.withActions is true.
Replies are parsed for multiple [[...]] directives, which are stripped from the visible text.
Supported verbs include teach, behaviour, facet, forget, dial, generate, refine, decide, and stream.
apply() writes through real registries and shows toast notifications via MS_TOAST.
generate/refine/decide actions use MS_PRODUCE when available, falling back to MS_ARTIFACTS for bare creation.
Custom events vi-artifact-made and vi-run-stream are dispatched to notify other modules.
A React hook useViChat is exposed separately for UI components to subscribe to the store.
findArtifact performs loose name matching and falls back to the most recent artifact when the hint is vague.
standouts: Unifies all Vi conversation surfaces into one shared, reload-surviving message thread.
Parses and executes multiple inline agent directives within a single reply.
Directly mutates project state by dispatching parsed chat directives to production/artifact registries.
conventions: Uses global window.* namespace modules rather than ES imports.
Wraps the module in an IIFE and attaches it to window.
Uses defensive optional-access patterns such as window.MS_FEEDBACK && window.MS_FEEDBACK.addLearning.
Persists state as JSON in localStorage and silently catches storage parse/serialize errors.
Communicates across modules via CustomEvent dispatches in addition to direct registry calls.
concerns: Relies on many global window.* APIs whose contracts are not defined in this file.
parseOne splits on | and = without escaping, so directive values containing those characters will break.
apply() has no validation of directive fields and no rollback mechanism.
Actions are applied automatically without any user confirmation visible in this file.
send() prepends the system prompt as user content rather than using a separate system role.
No cancellation or abort handling for the pending window.claude.complete call.
findArtifact falling back to the most recent artifact could target the wrong part.
Error handling in apply is minimal; missing registries silently do nothing.
notes: This is the shared chat engine: any UI that talks to Vi should read and write here instead of keeping its own thread.
questions: Which surfaces currently consume this store and how do they initialize?
Where is window.claude defined and what is the exact response shape of complete()?
What is the full shape of MS_VI_BRAIN.genContext() and systemPrompt()?
Are actions intended to be auto-applied, or should a surface confirm them first?
What kinds are available in MS_KINDS for the generate directive?
How is the busy flag surfaced to users, and can an in-flight request be cancelled?
What is the lifecycle relationship between MS_ARTIFACTS.create and MS_PRODUCE.create?
purpose_vs_actual: Matches: the file states it unifies conversation state, persists it, owns the turn loop, and applies actions through real registries, and the code does those things.
apparent_themes: Single source of truth for conversation state
Conversation as a mechanism for project mutation
Loose coupling through global registries and custom events
intention_signals: The file is trying to make talking to Vi one continuous, stateful, action-capable conversation that any surface can join, so chat turns can directly reshape the design project.
novelty: A chat store that doubles as an agentic action dispatcher, parsing multiple inline directives from a single reply and immediately writing through production and artifact registries.
contribution: A reusable, surface-agnostic conversation core that combines message persistence, prompt composition, multi-directive parsing, and project-side action execution into one module any Vi UI can consume.
summary_for_embedding: vi-chat.js is a global IIFE chat store and turn-loop for Vi that persists messages, composes prompts, parses inline action directives, and applies them through project registries.
intention_for_embedding: Intended to unify every Vi conversation surface into one continuous, stateful, action-capable thread that can directly mutate the design project.

symbols.does: Saves the last 40 messages to localStorage and notifies subscribers.
symbols.description: Serializes state.messages.slice(-40) under the key ms_vi_chat_v1, swallows exceptions, then calls every subscriber function so surfaces can re-render.

symbols.does: Restores messages from localStorage on startup.
symbols.description: Reads the same storage key, parses JSON, validates that messages is an array, and assigns it to state.messages; failures are silently ignored.

symbols.does: Registers a callback to be called on state changes.
symbols.description: Adds the function to a Set and returns an unsubscribe function that removes it.

symbols.does: Returns the current chat state object.
symbols.description: Exposes the internal state reference containing the messages array and the busy flag.

symbols.does: Clears messages and the busy flag, then persists.
symbols.description: Empties the conversation history, resets the busy indicator, and notifies subscribers.
Creates a new messages array containing the added message, then calls persist to save and broadcast.

symbols.does: Locates an artifact by a loose name hint for refine/decide actions.
symbols.description: Searches MS_ARTIFACTS.all() using exact, contains, or kind-label matches and falls back to the most recent artifact when the hint is empty or vague.

symbols.does: Parses a single [[...]] directive body into a verb plus key/value map.
symbols.description: Splits the body on | and then on the first = to build an action object; it does not handle embedded delimiters.

symbols.does: Extracts all directives from a reply and returns cleaned text plus an action list.
symbols.description: Uses a global regex to collect every [[...]] block, parses each with parseOne, removes the directives from the visible text, and collapses extra blank lines.

symbols.does: Back-compat helper that returns the first directive only.
symbols.description: Wraps parseActions and exposes the first action as action for older callers.

symbols.does: Produces a human-readable summary of an action for UI display.
symbols.description: Maps each supported verb to a short label, looking up related records in MS_IDENTITY, MS_MEMORY, MS_DECISIONS, MS_KINDS, MS_STREAMS, or MS_ARTIFACTS as needed.

symbols.does: Executes one parsed action through the real project registries.
symbols.description: Branches on verb to write feedback, identity behaviours or facets, memory, decision dials, artifact generation, refinement, approval, or stream events, showing toast notifications and dispatching custom events where appropriate.

symbols.does: Runs a list of actions and returns how many were applied.
symbols.description: Iterates over the actions array, calls apply for each, and counts those that return truthy.

symbols.does: Shows a toast notification if MS_TOAST is available.
Handles a user turn: stores the message, calls Claude, parses actions, and stores the reply.
symbols.description: Pushes the user message, sets busy, builds a system prompt from MS_VI_BRAIN or a fallback, appends the action protocol when opts.withActions is true, awaits window.claude.complete, parses directives, and pushes Vi's cleaned reply with attached actions.
